entity: Working in the Time of COVID-19 Oral History Project
surface_forms: Working in the Time of COVID-19 Oral History Project
description_wiki: The global COVID-19 pandemic (also known as the coronavirus pandemic), caused by severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus 2 (SARS-CoV-2), began with an outbreak in Wuhan, China, in December 2019.

description: archive containing more than 300 separate collections of labor and labor-related materials from individuals and organizations documenting the local, national, and international dimensions of the labor movement in the Pacific Northwest
description_wiki: The Labor Archives of Washington is a special collection at the University of Washington Libraries dedicated to preserving documents from the labor movement in Washington state.

description: city in and county seat of King County, State of Washington, United States
description_wiki: Seattle (  see-AT-əl) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Washington and the Pacific Northwest region of North America.
Korea is a peninsular region in East Asia consisting of the Korean Peninsula, Jeju Island, and smaller islands.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Montana (  mon-TAN-ə) is a landlocked state in the Mountain West subregion of the Western United States.

description: largest Christian Church, made up of 24 autonomous Churches in communion with the Pope, part of the Latin Church aka Latin Christianity aka western Christianity
description_wiki: The Catholic Church (Latin: Ecclesia Catholica), commonly called the Roman Catholic Church, is the largest Christian church, with an estimated 1.28 to 1.41 billion baptized members worldwide as of 2026.
Harborview Medical Center is a public hospital located in the First Hill neighborhood of Seattle, Washington, United States.

description: extracorporeal technique of providing both cardiac and respiratory support
description_wiki: Extracorporeal membrane oxygenation (ECMO) is a form of extracorporeal life support, operated by a perfusionist, providing prolonged cardiac and respiratory support to people whose heart and lungs are unable to provide an adequate amount of oxygen, gas exchange or blood supply (perfusion) to sustain life.

description: North American trade union which represents 2 millions worker from USA, Canada, Puerto Rico & focused on healthcare, public services, property service
description_wiki: Service Employees International Union (SEIU) is a labor union representing 2 million workers in over 100 occupations in the United States, Canada, and Puerto Rico.

The Pacific Northwest (PNW; French: Nord-Ouest Pacifique) is a geographic region in Western North America bounded by its coastal waters of the Pacific Ocean to the west and, loosely, by the Rocky Mountains to the east.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Alaska (  ə-LASS-kə) is a non-contiguous U.S. state located in the northwestern regions of North America.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Idaho ( , EYE-də-hoe) is a landlocked state in the Pacific Northwest and Mountain West subregions of the Western United States.

description: form of physical trauma sustained from the discharge of arms or munitions
description_wiki: A gunshot wound (GSW) is a penetrating injury caused by a projectile (e.g., a bullet) shot from a gun.

description: penetration of a victim with a sharp or pointed object at close range
description_wiki: A stabbing is penetration or rough contact with a sharp or pointed object at close range.

The global COVID-19 pandemic (also known as the coronavirus pandemic), caused by severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus 2 (SARS-CoV-2), began with an outbreak in Wuhan, China, in December 2019.

description: protective clothing or equipment designed to help protect an individual from injury, Noise, infection, or other hazards
description_wiki: Personal protective equipment (PPE) is protective clothing, helmets, goggles, or other garments or equipment designed to protect the wearer's body from injury or infection.

description: public research university in Seattle, Washington, United States
description_wiki: The University of Washington (UW, and informally U-Dub or U Dub) is a public research university in Seattle, Washington, United States.
